title: Bromelia neotenuifolia
taxonomy: Bromelia neotenuifolia is classified within the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This family comprises over 50 genera of flowering plants, primarily found in the Americas. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Bromelia neotenuifolia is an epiphytic or terrestrial plant with adaptations that allow it to thrive in a variety of environments. The species is further classified within the genus Bromelia, which includes around 100 species of plants native to the Americas. The classification of Bromelia neotenuifolia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Bromeliaceae family.

intro: Bromelia neotenuifolia, also known as the "Golden Pineapple Plant", is a species of flowering plant in the Bromeliad family, characterized by its striking yellow and green foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Bromelia neotenuifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
